category_title: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches
prompt: A three-branch fixture head for a bench machine. A central mast carries three radial arms on separate hub blocks at staggered heights, each ending in a small mounting pad. Each arm is supported by its own revolute joint about a local hub axis, with the three branches arranged so their swept volumes stay distinct.
label: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches Realism 1

category_title: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches
prompt: A two-branch positioning fixture. A rigid central spine carries an upper branch arm and a lower branch arm on separate side-supported hubs, with a small tooling pad at each tip. The two branches are independent revolute joints about parallel axes, and each joint should read as its own supported shaft and bearing set.
label: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches Realism 1

prompt: A cantilever support arm for bench tooling. A base column carries a shoulder link, an elbow link, and a short wrist plate held in clevis brackets. Shoulder and elbow are revolute joints about parallel horizontal axes, and the wrist is a separate revolute joint about the tool axis.
label: Cantilever articulated arm Realism 1

prompt: A compact rotary indexing stack. Nested circular stages sit on one vertical shaft with a base turntable, a middle ring, and a top flange for tooling. The base, middle, and top stages are separate revolute joints about the shared vertical axis, each tied to a visible bearing support or hub interface.
label: Coaxial rotary stack Realism 1

prompt: A two-finger gripper mechanism on a shared palm block. Each finger is a three-link phalanx chain with tapered rigid links, pin knuckles, and a separate base pivot mounted side by side. Each finger chain has three revolute joints bending in one plane, and the two fingers remain mechanically independent rather than coupled.
label: Dual independent finger chains Realism 1

prompt: A dual-column lift module like a compact pallet stacker mast. Two vertical guide columns are tied by a base and top crosshead, and a rectangular carriage rides between them on wrapped guide blocks. One prismatic joint moves the carriage along the column axis while the frame stays fixed.
label: Dual-column lift carriage Realism 1

prompt: A gantry positioning axis for a small industrial machine. Two long base rails support a bridge carriage, and a tool truck rides across the bridge beam on its own short slide. The bridge is a prismatic joint along the rail axis, and the tool truck is a second prismatic joint along the orthogonal beam axis.
label: Dual-rail gantry axis Realism 1

prompt: A single articulated finger mechanism. A base knuckle carries three tapered phalanx links with side plates and exposed pin joints, ending in a small pad. The three links are connected by revolute joints that all bend in one plane from the supported knuckle barrels.
label: Finger-like phalanx chain Realism 1

prompt: A folding support arm like the linkage on a task light or small gate stay. A base bracket carries three flat bar links in series and ends in a small attachment pad. The chain has three revolute joints about parallel axes in one plane so the arm can collapse near the base and extend outward.
label: Folding arm chain Realism 1

prompt: A serial linkage made from five rigid bars. The root link mounts to a fixed base foot, and the remaining links connect through evenly spaced hinge blocks. All four joints are revolute about parallel axes in one plane.
label: Four-joint revolute chain Realism 1

prompt: A right-angle gearbox assembly with an exposed train. A base bracket carries a vertical input shaft with a bevel gear, a horizontal output shaft with the mating bevel gear, and a secondary spur reduction on the output side. Each shaft is a revolute joint about its supported bearing axis, and every gear should sit on a clear shaft and housing support.
label: Gear Assemblies Realism 1

prompt: A panel-mounted industrial joystick mechanism. A boxed base supports an outer gimbal ring, an inner yoke, and a short control lever with a mounting cap. The outer ring rotates in pitch about a horizontal axis, and the inner yoke rotates in roll about an orthogonal axis through the same support center.
label: Joystick mechanism Realism 1

prompt: A three-link lever chain for a compact mechanical linkage. A base lug supports three rigid lever bars in series and a small tab at the free end. All three joints are revolute about parallel axes in one plane.
label: Lever chain Realism 1

category_title: Linear carriage with independent rotary end-effector
prompt: A rail-mounted tool slide with a powered spindle head. A base rail carries a moving carriage, and the carriage supports a short spindle in its own bearing housing. The carriage is a prismatic joint along the rail axis, and the spindle is a separate revolute joint about its local tool axis.
label: Linear carriage with independent rotary end-effector Realism 1

prompt: A compact forklift mast section. Two upright channels and a top crosshead support a lift carriage with a load backrest and paired forks. One vertical prismatic joint moves the carriage on the mast rails while the forks remain rigid to the carriage.
label: Mast-mounted fork carriage Realism 1

prompt: A desk-mounted monitor arm shown without a screen. A clamp base carries a lower arm, an upper arm, and a compact VESA head with a tilt bracket. The shoulder, elbow, and head swivel are revolute joints about vertical axes, and the head tilt is a revolute joint about a horizontal axis at the mounting plate.
label: Monitor mount Realism 1

prompt: A compact fold-out support arm with four short segments. A fixed base plate carries four boxed links in series and a small platform bracket at the end. The four joints are revolute about parallel axes in one plane so the arm packs close to the base and unfolds into a longer reach.
label: Multi-segment fold-out arm Realism 1

prompt: An offset rotary fixture stack. A base turntable carries a raised side arm that supports a second rotary stage on a parallel axis displaced from the base axis, topped by a small flange. The lower and upper stages are separate revolute joints about their own supported vertical axes.
label: Offset-axis rotary stack Realism 1

prompt: A parallel-jaw gripper head. A central body carries left and right jaw carriages on mirrored guideways, and each carriage ends in a tall gripping finger. The two jaws are separate prismatic joints along the common closing axis, each supported by its own slide rails within the body.
label: Opposed twin-slide gripper Realism 1

prompt: A two-axis positioning stage. A base carries an X slide with a rectangular carriage, and that carriage supports a Y slide turned ninety degrees with a small top table. The X and Y stages are prismatic joints along orthogonal horizontal axes.
label: Orthogonal XY stage Realism 1

prompt: An XZ positioning stage for a lab fixture. A base rail carries a horizontal carriage, and that carriage supports a vertical slide with a small tool plate. The lower axis is a prismatic joint along the horizontal X direction, and the upper axis is a prismatic joint along the vertical Z direction.
label: Orthogonal XZ stage Realism 1

prompt: A side-mounted YZ positioning stage. A fixed frame carries a vertical carriage, and that carriage supports a second slide oriented sideways with a small end platform. One prismatic joint moves along the vertical Z axis, and the other prismatic joint moves along the horizontal Y axis.
label: Orthogonal YZ stage Realism 1

prompt: A two-axis wrist module for a compact robot. A side-supported pitch yoke carries a roll frame and a small faceplate at the center. Pitch is a revolute joint about a horizontal trunnion axis, and roll is a revolute joint about the orthogonal faceplate axis nested inside the yoke.
label: Pitch-roll module Realism 1

prompt: A portal gantry module for a routing or inspection machine. Two side frames support a bridge beam that travels on base rails, and a central carriage on the beam carries a vertical tool slide. The bridge is a prismatic joint along the base rails, the carriage is a prismatic joint along the beam, and the tool head is a prismatic joint along the vertical axis.
label: Portal gantry with vertical slide Realism 1

prompt: A two-joint actuator chain. A fixed base guide carries a sliding carriage, and the carriage supports a hinged output link at its front. The first joint is prismatic along the guide axis, and the second joint is revolute about a supported pin axis at the carriage.
label: Prismatic-revolute chain Realism 1

prompt: A three-joint linear and rotary transfer chain. A base guide carries a sliding carriage, the carriage supports a hinged middle arm, and the arm carries a short telescoping end slide. The joints are prismatic along the base guide, revolute about the carriage pin axis, and prismatic along the end-slide axis.
label: Prismatic-revolute-prismatic chain Realism 1

prompt: A carriage-fed two-link arm. A base rail supports a sliding carriage, the carriage carries a shoulder link, and the shoulder carries a short forearm link. The chain starts with a prismatic joint along the rail axis, followed by two revolute joints about supported pin axes at the shoulder and elbow.
label: Prismatic-revolute-revolute chain Realism 1

prompt: A push-pull plunger assembly like a latching rod mechanism. A cylindrical guide body supports a sliding plunger rod with a rear knob and a front clevis tip. The plunger is a single prismatic joint along the rod axis within the guide body.
label: Push-pull plunger chain Realism 1

prompt: A radial positioning arm for a drill or inspection fixture. A central column supports a horizontal arm that can swing around the column, and a tool carriage slides along the arm to a small head plate. The arm is a revolute joint about the column axis, and the carriage is a prismatic joint along the arm axis.
label: Radial arm with sliding carriage Realism 1

prompt: A swing-out slide mechanism. A base bracket carries a hinged primary arm, and the free end of that arm supports a short linear slide with a rectangular carriage. The first joint is revolute about the base pin axis, and the second joint is prismatic along the slide axis mounted to the arm.
label: Revolute-prismatic chain Realism 1

prompt: A hinged transfer arm with an extending wrist. A base pivot carries a primary arm, the arm contains a sliding carriage, and the carriage supports a small hinged end plate. The chain has a revolute joint at the base, a prismatic joint along the arm, and a final revolute joint at the wrist plate.
label: Revolute-prismatic-revolute chain Realism 1

prompt: A two-link arm with a sliding tip stage. A base bracket supports a shoulder link, the shoulder carries a forearm link, and the forearm contains a short linear end slide. The first two joints are revolute about supported pin axes, and the last joint is prismatic along the forearm tip axis.
label: Revolute-revolute-prismatic chain Realism 1

prompt: A rotary pedestal with a lifting head. A circular base turntable carries a vertical guide column, and a carriage on that column holds a small tooling plate. The base is a revolute joint about the vertical pedestal axis, and the carriage is a prismatic joint along the column axis.
label: Rotary base with vertical slide Realism 1

prompt: A machine-tool trunnion table. A heavy base supports a rotary table on one axis, and the table assembly is carried between side frames on a second axis for tilt. Table rotation is a revolute joint about its central table axis, and tilt is a revolute joint about the trunnion axis through the side supports.
label: Rotary table with tilting trunnion Realism 1

prompt: A simple two-link elbow arm. A fixed base carries an upper arm link, and the upper arm supports a forearm link ending in a small tool pad. The shoulder and elbow are revolute joints about parallel horizontal axes.
label: Serial elbow arm Realism 1

prompt: A compact robotic arm. A base pedestal supports a shoulder joint, an upper arm link, an elbow joint, a forearm link, and a wrist head with a round tool flange. The shoulder rotates about a vertical axis at the pedestal, the elbow rotates about a horizontal axis at the arm knuckle, and the wrist is a revolute joint about the tool axis.
label: Shoulder-elbow-wrist arm Realism 1

prompt: A supported rotary shaft module. A straight shaft runs through two bearing blocks on a base plate and ends in a small flange or pulley hub. The shaft is one continuous revolute joint about its own supported axis.
label: Single continuous rotary shaft Realism 1

prompt: A single-axis pitch cradle. Two side plates on a base support a centered yoke carrying a small faceplate between the arms. The yoke rotates as one revolute joint about a horizontal trunnion axis through the side supports.
label: Single pitch axis module Realism 1

prompt: A linear slide module. A fixed guide rail supports a rectangular carriage with a small mounting plate on top. The carriage is a single prismatic joint along the guide axis.
label: Single prismatic slider Realism 1

prompt: A heavy-duty leaf hinge. Two rectangular leaves connect through alternating knuckles around a central pin barrel. The hinge has one revolute joint along the barrel axis.
label: Single revolute hinge Realism 1

prompt: A single-axis roll stage. A compact housing supports a short spindle or faceplate on bearings, with a mounting foot below the housing. The spindle is one revolute joint about its longitudinal axis through the bearing housing.
label: Single roll axis module Realism 1

prompt: A single-axis yaw turntable. A round base supports a rotating upper plate on a centered hub and bearing stack, with a small payload bracket above. The upper plate is one revolute joint about the vertical center axis.
label: Single yaw axis module Realism 1

prompt: A rectangular telescoping boom for lifting or inspection equipment. Nested box-section stages extend from a fixed root mount, with a small head bracket at the tip. Each stage slides prismatically along the boom axis within the stage that contains it.
label: Telescoping boom Realism 1

prompt: A telescoping inspection mast with a rotating top head. Nested mast tubes rise from a fixed base frame, and the top stage carries a compact pan head with a mounting plate. The mast stages are prismatic joints along the vertical mast axis, and the head is a revolute joint about the same vertical axis at the top support.
label: Telescoping mast with pan head Realism 1

prompt: A three-stage linear slide chain. A fixed base guide carries a first carriage, that carriage carries a second guide, and the second carriage carries a third short guide with an end plate. All three joints are prismatic and each one slides along its own supported guide axis.
label: Three-joint prismatic chain Realism 1

prompt: A three-joint serial hinge chain. A base link supports two intermediate links and a short end link through offset hinge blocks. All three joints are revolute about parallel axes in one plane.
label: Three-joint revolute chain Realism 1

prompt: A three-stage telescoping drawer-style slide. Three nested channel sections extend from a fixed outer frame and end in a moving tray plate. The inner and middle sections are prismatic joints sliding along the same axis within their surrounding channels.
label: Three-stage telescoping slide Realism 1

prompt: A full-motion TV wall mount shown without a screen. A wall plate connects to a folding two-link arm, and the arm ends in a rectangular head frame with separate swivel and tilt support pieces. The arm joints and head swivel are revolute joints about vertical axes, and the head tilt is a revolute joint about a horizontal axis at the frame.
label: TV wall mount Realism 1

prompt: A two-stage linear slide. A fixed outer guide carries a first carriage, and that carriage carries a second short guide with an end plate. Both joints are prismatic along supported guide axes.
label: Two-joint prismatic chain Realism 1

prompt: A two-link hinged arm. A base bracket supports a primary link, and the primary link supports a secondary link with a small end pad. The two joints are revolute about parallel pin axes in one plane.
label: Two-joint revolute chain Realism 1

prompt: A two-stage telescoping slide like a heavy drawer runner. A fixed outer channel carries an inner sliding channel and a small mounting tray at the moving end. The inner channel is a prismatic joint along the slide axis within the outer channel.
label: Two-stage telescoping slide Realism 1

prompt: A louver-style vane bank. A rectangular frame holds several parallel slats, each supported at both ends by its own pivot stubs. Each vane is an independent revolute joint about its long axis within the side frame.
label: Vane array with independent pivots Realism 1

prompt: A vertical tool slide with a hinged head. A rigid column supports a moving carriage, and the carriage carries a short wrist plate between side brackets. The carriage is a prismatic joint along the vertical guide axis, and the wrist plate is a revolute joint about a horizontal hinge axis at the carriage front.
label: Vertical slide with wrist hinge Realism 1

prompt: A three-axis Cartesian positioning stage. A base carries an X-axis bridge, the bridge carries a Y-axis carriage, and the carriage carries a Z-axis slide with a small tool plate. X, Y, and Z are prismatic joints along three orthogonal supported guide axes.
label: XYZ Cartesian stage Realism 1

prompt: A two-axis gimbal module. A yaw turntable base carries a U-shaped fork, and the fork supports a pitch cradle with a small faceplate between its arms. Yaw is a revolute joint about the vertical base axis, and pitch is a revolute joint about the horizontal fork axis.
label: Yaw-pitch module Realism 1

prompt: A three-axis robotic wrist. A yaw base carries a pitch yoke, and the pitch yoke carries a roll spindle with a round tool flange at the tip. Yaw is a revolute joint about a vertical axis, pitch is a revolute joint about a horizontal axis inside the yoke, and roll is a revolute joint about the tool axis at the spindle.
label: Yaw-pitch-roll wrist Realism 1
